title: astrantia pauciflora subsp. tenorei
intro: Astrantia pauciflora subsp. tenorei is a subspecies of flowering plant in the genus Astrantia, commonly known as the few-flowered masterwort. It is a perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its white or pink flowers with a prominent yellow center. The medicinal properties of Astrantia pauciflora subsp. tenorei include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, which are often used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions to promote relaxation, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
